title: Chapter 23: A New Dawn
content: The reforms began the morning after the bonding ceremony.

Sera had expected resistance — decades of tradition didn't disappear overnight, and the noble houses had built their power on the backs of the wolf-less caste. But something had shifted in the pack since the battle. Wolves who had fought side by side against the Shadow didn't care about bloodlines anymore. The warrior who saved your life was your brother, whether he came from a noble house or a servant's hovel.

"The Wolf-less Caste Abolition Act," Kael announced at the first council meeting after the ceremony. "Effective immediately. There is no such thing as a 'wolf-less' wolf. Every member of this pack who cannot transform will be given the same rights, the same opportunities, and the same respect as every other member."
The vote was unanimous.

"The Servant Quarters Improvement Initiative," Sera added, reading from her own notes. "Real beds. Warmer fires. Access to training if desired. No more twelve-hour shifts for kitchen staff. And wages — actual wages, not just room and board."
"The treasury can support it," Darius said, surprising everyone. "Aldric's seized assets give us a surplus. And the Vane family has 'volunteered' a substantial contribution."
"Volunteered?" Sera raised an eyebrow.
"They were very... persuasive." Darius's grey eyes glinted. "I explained that voluntary contributions would look much better than mandatory fines at their next council review."
"Diplomatic."
"I have my moments."

The reforms rolled out over the following weeks. It wasn't seamless — there were grumbles from the noble houses, awkward moments when former servants sat at the same tables as their former masters, and at least one fistfight in the great hall when a young noble made the mistake of calling a former kitchen maid a derogatory name within earshot of Darius.
But the pack adapted. Wolves were resilient creatures. They'd survived a madman's army and an ancient shadow entity — they could survive eating lunch with the help.
Sera spent her days in council meetings and her nights in Kael's arms, and somewhere in between, she found time to be happy.
Three months after the bonding ceremony, Sera stood in the Moon Glade under a waxing moon, her hand pressed to her belly.
She'd known for a week. The healers had confirmed it that morning — a heartbeat, small and steady, growing inside her. Not one heartbeat. Two.
"Twins," she whispered to the moonlight. "Silver and black."
Kael found her there an hour later. The bond had told him something was wrong — or not wrong, but *significant*. He'd felt her emotions spike and then settle into something quiet and glowing.
"Sera?" He emerged from the tree line, his golden eyes scanning her face. "What is it? Is everything — "
"We're having pups."
He stopped walking. "What?"
"Twins." She turned to face him, her smile threatening to split her face. "The healers confirmed it this morning. Two heartbeats. Two pups."

Kael crossed the distance between them in three strides and swept her into his arms. His face was buried in her hair, and she could feel him shaking — not with fear, but with joy so intense it overwhelmed even his legendary control.
"Twins," he said, his voice muffled. "We're having twins."
"Silver and black, probably. Like the prophecy said."
"The prophecy didn't say anything about pups."
"It implied it." She pulled back to look at his face. "You're crying."
"I'm not crying."
"You're absolutely crying. There are tears on your face. I can see them."
"Those are... moisture. From the trees. The forest is very moist tonight."

*"I'M CRYING. I'M CRYING SO HARD. PUPS. TWINS. TWO TINY LITTLE WOLVES THAT ARE HALF HER AND HALF US. THIS IS THE GREATEST MOMENT OF MY LIFE. BETTER THAN THE TRIAL. BETTER THAN THE BATTLE. BETTER THAN THE WEDDING. PUPS. PUUUUUPPPPSSSS."*
"Fenris is howling," Sera reported.
"He's been howling since the word 'twins.'" Kael pressed his forehead to hers. "I love you. I love them. I love everything about this moment."
"I love you too." She kissed him — soft and warm and full of promise. "We're going to be parents."
"The most terrifying words I've ever heard."
"More terrifying than 'ancient shadow entity'?"
"Much more." But he was smiling — the real smile, the one that made his golden eyes crinkle at the corners. "I can't wait."
